settings.title: Brand Identity for Saltbox Coffee Roasters
settings.summary: We built a full identity and bag system for a roaster opening its first three cafes.
settings.description:
Saltbox was moving from wholesale into its own cafes and had a logo that did not hold up at small sizes or on a coffee bag. It had been drawn for a letterhead, and it turned to mush on a cup or across a shop window.

The brief

This was a bigger moment than a new logo. Saltbox was opening three cafes at once and putting its name in front of walk-in customers for the first time. They needed an identity that worked on a bag in a hand, a sign over a door, and a cup on a table, all at the same time.

What we did

We started with a new wordmark drawn to stay legible from a phone-sized retail tile up to a storefront sign. From there we built a flexible label system that codes each roast by color, so a regular can spot their usual bag without reading the fine print, and designed the signage and cup art for the first locations.

  • A wordmark that holds up from small bags to large signage
  • A roast label system color-coded by blend
  • Cafe signage for the first three locations
  • Cup and bag art tying the cafe and the shelf together

Opening three cafes on one timeline was the real test of the system. Everything had to be specified tightly enough that three fit-outs could roll out in parallel without drifting apart, so the third cafe looks like the first.

The result

The roaster launched all three cafes on a single visual language, and the bags now read clearly from across the shelf. The color-coded roasts make the wholesale lineup easier to shop too, which mattered since that side of the business did not go away.
